name: AARP Livable Communities Map
notes: List of projects funded by AARP to make communities more livable for people of all ages

name: AllTransit Maps and Metrics
notes: Analyzes transit quality through the social and economic impacts of transit

name: Appalachian Trail Interactive Map
notes: Maps shelters (with photos), vistas, and other assets along the Appalachian Trail

name: ArcGIS Living Atlas of the World
notes: Super comprehensive collection of different mapping data from around the world

name: Candid – Search Organizations
notes: Search nonprofits to understand their funding

name: Car Access Data (National Equity Atlas)
notes: Sub-resource of National Equity Atlas that examines car access

name: Data.gov
notes: Government data clearinghouse

name: DataStore – NPS Core Spatial Data Standard (Template & Document)
notes: NPS Core Spacial Data Standard

name: Dept. of Commerce – National Travel and Tourism Office Research Portal
notes: Range of reports, dashboards, and profiles on US travel data

name: FEMA Indices and Analytical Tools
notes: CRAZY amount of information on risk, climate resilience, vulnerability, etc.

name: Flood Potential Portal
notes: Lots of data

name: Forest Service Maps Page
notes: FSGeodata Clearinghouse has directory to all USFS maps. Interactive Visitor Map 2.0 is incredibly comprehensive for trails and trailheads!

name: H+T Affordability Index
notes: Housing and transportation affordability

name: Large-Scale Solar Projects Map
notes: Database and interactive map of all large-scale solar energy facilities in the U.S.

name: National Land Cover Database (NLCD)
notes: Data that describe nationwide land cover and land change over nearly four decades

name: National Water and Recreation Trails Map
notes: Filterable map of all water and recreation trails

name: Nonprofit Spending Research Guide
notes: Library of Congress research guide on nonprofits

name: Racial Equity Data Lab
notes: Helps with creating visualizations; sub-resource of National Equity Atlas

name: Rails to Trails Conservancy
notes: Rail trail maps, research projects, BikeAble planner, etc.

name: TRAILS Decision Support Tool (USGS)
notes: The trail planning tool (TRAILS) is only available on request. Target users are those involved in the initial planning of new trails or improvements to existing trail routes or systems that would eventually be available in the public domain. Typically, participants work either directly for government land management agencies or partner with government land management agencies.

name: The Nature Conservancy Resilient Land Mapping Tool
notes: For conservation planning with climate change in mind

name: U.N. Tourism Data Dashboard
notes: Statistics and insights on key indicators for inbound and outbound tourism at global, regional, and national levels

name: Work Zone Data Initiative
notes: Enables infrastructure owners and operators (IOOs) to make harmonized work zone data available for third party use.
